general.title: 1. General Information
general.text1: At Lopes2Tech, we take the protection of your personal data very seriously. We treat your personal data confidentially and in accordance with the statutory data protection regulations—specifically the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP/nDSG) and, where applicable, the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
general.text2: This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your data when you visit our website or use our services.

controller.title: 2. Responsible Person (Controller)
controller.text: The responsible party for data processing on this website is:

serverLogs.title: 3.1 Server Log Files
serverLogs.text: When you access our website, the provider of the pages automatically collects and stores information in so-called 'server log files,' including:
serverLogs.note: This data is not merged with other data sources. The processing is based on our legitimate interest in the technically error-free presentation and optimization of our website.

contact.title: 3.2 Contact Form and Email
contact.text: If you send us inquiries via the contact form or email, your details from the inquiry form, including the contact details you provided there (Name, Email, Phone, Company/Practice), will be stored by us for the purpose of processing the inquiry and in case of follow-up questions. We do not pass on this data without your consent.

booking.title: 3.3 Appointment Booking (Cal.com)
booking.text: We use the service Cal.com to offer online appointment booking. When you book an appointment, the data you enter (Name, Email, Phone, Reason for Appointment) is processed by Cal.com.
booking.note: Please check Cal.com's privacy policy for details on how they handle data.

chatbot.title: 3.4 AI Chatbot Assistant
chatbot.text: Our website utilizes an AI-powered chatbot to assist with inquiries. When you interact with the chat:

list.process: The text you input is processed to generate a response.
list.warning: Important: Do not enter sensitive health data (patient names, medical conditions) into the public website chatbot.
list.providers: Data may be processed by third-party AI providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) solely for the purpose of generating the answer.

cookies.title: 4. Cookies
cookies.text: Our website uses 'cookies.' These are small text files that are stored on your device.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.text: Necessary for the technical operation of the website.

analytical.title: Analytical Cookies
analytical.text: Help us understand how visitors use the site.

cookies.note: You can configure your browser to reject cookies, but this may limit the functionality of the website.

analytics.title: 5. Analytics and Third-Party Tools
analytics.text: To improve our service, we may use analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics or Vercel Analytics). These tools use cookies to analyze user behavior. The IP addresses are typically anonymized before processing. You can opt-out of this tracking via our Cookie Banner or your browser settings.

security.title: 6. Data Security (SSL/TLS)
security.text: For security reasons and to protect the transmission of confidential content, such as orders or inquiries you send to us, this site uses SSL or TLS encryption. You can recognize an encrypted connection by the fact that the address line of the browser changes from 'http://' to 'https://' and by the lock symbol in your browser line.

transfer.title: 7. Data Transfer to Third Parties and Abroad
transfer.text: We only pass on your data to third parties if this is necessary for the fulfillment of the contract (e.g., hosting, scheduling tools) or if you have consented. Some of these service providers may be located outside of Switzerland (e.g., USA). In these cases, we ensure that appropriate data protection safeguards (such as Standard Contractual Clauses) are in place to comply with Swiss nDSG and GDPR standards.

rights.title: 8. Your Rights

list.info: Request information about your stored personal data at any time.
list.correction: Request the correction of incorrect data.
list.deletion: Request the deletion of your data (Right to be forgotten), provided there are no legal retention obligations.
list.revocation: Revoke your consent to data processing at any time.

rights.contact: To exercise these rights, please contact us directly at

changes.title: 9. Changes to this Privacy Policy
changes.text: We reserve the right to adapt this privacy policy so that it always complies with current legal requirements or to implement changes to our services in the privacy policy (e.g., when introducing new services). The new privacy policy will then apply to your next visit.

conclusion.title: 3. Conclusion of Contract
conclusion.text: Offers made by the Contractor are valid for 30 days unless otherwise stated. The contract is concluded upon:

list.acceptance: The written acceptance of the offer by the Client (email is sufficient).
list.deposit: The payment of an initial deposit.
list.commencement: The commencement of actual service provision by the Contractor with the Client's knowledge.

list.content: Provide all necessary content (text, images, logos) in a timely manner.
list.access: Grant the necessary access rights (hosting, API keys, domain registrar) required for the project.
list.review: Review deliverables and provide feedback within 5 working days. If no feedback is received within this period, the deliverable is deemed accepted.

projectServices.title: 5.1 Project Services
projectServices.text: Unless otherwise agreed in the individual offer, the following payment terms apply:

small.title: Small Projects
small.subtitle: Up to CHF 2,500
small.text: 100% of the project fee is due immediately upon conclusion of the contract.

standard.title: Standard & Large Projects
standard.subtitle: Above CHF 2,500
standard.deposit: 50% Deposit: Due immediately to reserve resources.

projectServices.terms: Invoices are payable within 20 days net.

recurring.title: 5.2 Recurring Services
recurring.text: Fees for ongoing maintenance or SaaS components (e.g., Chatbot hosting) are billed monthly or annually in advance. The cancellation period for recurring services is 30 days to the end of the month, unless a minimum contract term was agreed upon.

list.beforeWork: The Client may cancel the contract before work begins and receive a full deposit refund.
list.afterWork: If work has already started, the Client is obligated to pay for all work completed to date, calculated at the hourly rate agreed upon or a pro-rated portion of the total fee.
list.contractorTermination: The Contractor may terminate the contract if the Client fails to provide necessary materials or feedback for more than 30 days despite reminders.

list.rights: Upon full payment of the agreed fee, the Client receives the usage rights for the created software/website.
list.reuse: The Contractor retains the right to reuse generic code modules, libraries, and design patterns developed during the project for other clients (standard software engineering practice).
list.guarantee: The Client guarantees that all materials provided to the Contractor (images, texts) do not violate third-party rights.

ai.title: 7. Specific Provisions for AI Services
ai.text: The Client acknowledges that services involving Artificial Intelligence (AI) (e.g., Chatbots, RAG systems, Voice Agents) depend on third-party providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Retell AI).

accuracy.label: Accuracy:
accuracy.text: The Contractor cannot guarantee that AI outputs are 100% accurate. The Client is responsible for verifying critical information generated by AI.

availability.label: Availability:
availability.text: The Contractor is not liable for service interruptions caused by third-party API failures (e.g., if ChatGPT is down).

costs.label: Costs:
costs.text: Unless strictly included in a flat fee, variable API usage costs (token usage) may be billed to the Client or paid directly by the Client to the provider.

warranty.title: 8.1 Warranty
warranty.text: The Contractor warrants that the software/website is functional and free of critical defects at the time of acceptance. The warranty period is 3 months from delivery.
warranty.note: The warranty does not cover defects caused by third-party updates (e.g., a WordPress plugin update breaking the site) or unauthorized changes by the Client.
warranty.postWarranty: After the warranty period expires, bug fixes and updates are provided under the active maintenance plan or billed hourly at CHF 120/hour.

liability.title: 8.2 Liability
liability.text: To the extent permitted by Swiss law, the Contractor's liability is limited to intent and gross negligence. Liability for slight negligence, indirect damages, or consequential damages (e.g., loss of profit, data loss) is excluded.
liability.note: The Contractor is not liable for data privacy violations caused by the Client's misuse of the provided tools (e.g., entering patient data into a non-secure field).

forceMajeure.title: 8.3 Force Majeure
forceMajeure.text: Neither party shall be held liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from events beyond their reasonable control, including but not limited to natural disasters, pandemics, wars, strikes, governmental actions, or infrastructure failures.

dataProtection.title: 9. Data Protection and Confidentiality
dataProtection.text: Both parties agree to treat all business secrets and confidential information obtained during the business relationship as strictly confidential. The Contractor processes personal data in accordance with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nDSG).
dataProtection.retention: Project files, backups, and related data are retained for 12 months after project completion. Clients are responsible for downloading and archiving their project files before this period expires.

severability.title: Severability
severability.text: Should individual provisions of these GTC be invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions shall remain unaffected.

jurisdiction.text: The exclusive place of jurisdiction for all disputes arising from this contract is Zurich, Switzerland.

webDev.title: Professional Websites
webDev.desc1: Your website is your most powerful business asset — and first impressions are permanent. We design and develop custom, high-performance websites built from the ground up for Swiss businesses: fast, mobile-first, and engineered to convert visitors into clients.
webDev.desc2: We don’t use templates. Every project starts with your brand, your goals, and your audience. The result is a website that feels uniquely yours — one that builds trust the moment someone lands on it, and keeps them coming back.
webDev.bullet2: Mobile-first & optimized for Core Web Vitals

marketing.title: Digital Marketing
marketing.desc1: Being online isn’t enough — you need to be visible where your clients are. We build and execute data-driven digital marketing strategies that grow your audience, amplify your brand, and drive qualified leads directly to your business.
marketing.desc2: From targeted Meta and Instagram ad campaigns to organic content strategy and community management, every action we take is measured, refined, and aligned with your growth objectives. No vanity metrics — only results that matter.

seo.title: SEO Development
seo.desc1: Ranking on Google in Switzerland takes more than keywords — it takes a technically sound foundation that search engines understand and trust. We combine deep technical SEO expertise with content strategy to build sustainable, long-term organic visibility.
seo.desc2: From site architecture and Core Web Vitals to structured data markup and multilingual hreflang implementation, we cover every signal that determines where your business appears in search results. The goal isn’t traffic — it’s the right traffic.
seo.bullet1: Technical SEO audits & Core Web Vitals

automation.title: Business Automation
automation.desc1: Your team’s time is too valuable to spend on manual, repetitive tasks. We analyse your operations, identify the bottlenecks, and replace them with intelligent automations that run 24/7 — accurately, reliably, and without supervision.

faq.subtitle: Web Design in Switzerland
faq.q1: How much does a website cost in Switzerland?
faq.a1: Our website packages start from CHF 690 for a one-page site (Starter), CHF 1,390 for a 5-page professional site (Professional), and CHF 1,990 for up to 15 pages with 3+ languages and CMS (Business Pro). All packages include SSL, mobile-responsive design, Google Business Profile setup, and basic SEO.
faq.q2: How long does it take to build a website?
faq.a2: Starter websites are delivered in 4–5 days (rush option: 1 day for +CHF 100). Professional sites take about 2 weeks. Business Pro with CMS and multilingual setup typically takes 2–3 weeks depending on content availability.
faq.q3: Do you build websites in German, French, and English?
faq.a3: Yes! Multilingual websites are our specialty. The Professional package includes 2 languages (e.g. DE + EN), and Business Pro includes 3+ languages. We serve Swiss businesses in German, English, French, Italian, and Portuguese.
faq.q4: Is SEO included in the website price?
faq.a4: Basic SEO is included in every package — meta tags, sitemap generation, Google Search Console setup, Google Business Profile, and fast loading speeds. For ongoing monthly SEO improvement, we offer dedicated SEO packages.
faq.q5: Can I update my website myself after launch?
faq.a5: The Business Pro package includes a CMS (Content Management System) so you can update text, images, and blog posts yourself. For Starter and Professional, we handle updates as part of our monthly hosting plans (from CHF 39/month).
faq.q6: Do you serve Swiss businesses outside Zurich?
faq.a6: Absolutely! We work with businesses across all of Switzerland — Basel, Bern, Geneva, Lausanne, Lucerne, and beyond. All projects are managed remotely with video calls and fast online communication.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: SEO Services in Switzerland
faq.q1: How long does SEO take to show results in Switzerland?
faq.a1: Technical SEO improvements (Core Web Vitals, Schema markup, fixing crawl errors) can show results in 2–8 weeks. Content and authority building typically takes 3–6 months to meaningfully move rankings. We provide monthly reports so you always see progress.
faq.q2: What is technical SEO and why does it matter?
faq.a2: Technical SEO focuses on how search engines crawl and index your site — page speed, mobile usability, structured data, Core Web Vitals, and fixing crawl errors. Without a solid technical foundation, even great content won't rank.
faq.q3: Do you offer multilingual SEO for German, French, and Italian?
faq.a3: Yes! Switzerland's four language regions require dedicated multilingual SEO. We implement hreflang tags, localised meta data, and language-specific keyword strategies for German, French, English, Italian, and Portuguese.
faq.q4: What is Schema markup and how does it help?
faq.a4: Schema markup is structured data that tells Google exactly what your page is about. It enables rich results in search — star ratings, FAQs, prices, and business information — which significantly improves click-through rates.
faq.q5: Do you provide ongoing SEO or one-time audits?
faq.a5: Both. Our SEO Audit (CHF 490) is a one-time deep dive with actionable recommendations. For ongoing improvement, our SEO Starter (CHF 390/month) and SEO Growth (CHF 790/month) plans include continuous optimisation, reporting, and keyword tracking.
faq.q6: How do you measure SEO success?
faq.a6: We track keyword rankings, organic traffic, Core Web Vitals scores, click-through rates, and conversion events in Google Search Console and GA4. You get a monthly performance report with clear metrics and next steps.

faq.q1: What business processes can be automated?
faq.a1: Common automations include: lead capture → CRM → email follow-up sequences, appointment booking and reminders, invoice generation and payment tracking, customer onboarding flows, review request campaigns, and social media scheduling. If you do it repeatedly, we can likely automate it.

faq.a2: We use n8n, Make (formerly Integromat), Zapier, and custom Python scripts depending on your needs and budget. For complex business logic, we build custom APIs that tie your existing tools together seamlessly.
faq.q3: How long does it take to build an automation?
faq.a3: Simple workflows (connecting 2–3 tools) can be live within 1–2 days. Multi-step automations with error handling and monitoring typically take 1–2 weeks. We document every automation so your team can maintain it.
faq.q4: Will automation work with my existing software?
faq.a4: We connect over 5,000 apps including Google Workspace, HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive, Slack, WhatsApp Business, Calendly, and most CRM/ERP systems used in Switzerland.
faq.q5: What ROI can I expect from automation?
faq.a5: Most clients save 10–30 hours per month on repetitive tasks within the first month. A typical CHF 490 automation workflow pays for itself within the first week of use — and keeps saving every month after.
faq.q6: Do you train my team on the automations you build?
faq.a6: Yes! Every package includes full documentation and a training session so your team can monitor, adjust, and extend the workflows themselves. We also offer 30–60 day post-launch support to catch any edge cases.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: AI Integration in Switzerland
faq.q1: What is an AI chatbot and how can it help my business?
faq.a1: An AI chatbot is a 24/7 automated assistant trained on your business knowledge. It can answer customer questions, qualify leads, handle bookings, and escalate complex issues — all without human intervention. Swiss businesses use them to reduce support costs and respond faster.
faq.q2: What AI models do you work with?
faq.a2: We work with GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini, and open-source models like Llama depending on your requirements. For privacy-sensitive Swiss/EU deployments, we can set up fully on-premise or EU-hosted solutions with no data leaving Switzerland.
faq.q3: What is RAG and why does it matter?
faq.a3: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) lets the AI search through your own documents, FAQs, product catalogues, and knowledge base before answering. This means responses are accurate, up-to-date, and specific to your business — not generic AI hallucinations.
faq.q4: Can the AI integrate with my existing tools?
faq.a4: Yes! We integrate AI with your CRM, calendar, WhatsApp, email, Slack, Google Workspace, and custom APIs. The AI can read from and write to your existing systems — qualifying leads directly into HubSpot, for example.
faq.q5: How much does AI integration cost?
faq.a5: Our AI Chatbot package starts at CHF 890 (custom chatbot + 50-page knowledge base + website widget). AI Workflow (chatbot + email triage + CRM integration) is CHF 1,890. Custom AI with multi-agent orchestration starts from CHF 2,990.
faq.q6: Is our business data safe with AI?
faq.a6: Absolutely. For Swiss and EU compliance, we can deploy solutions that keep all data within Switzerland or the EU. We never use your business data to train general AI models, and we implement strict access controls and data encryption.

faq.q1: What is the difference between a website and a web app?
faq.a1: A website is primarily informational. A web app has interactive functionality: user authentication, data management, payments, role-based access, and complex business logic. Examples include SaaS dashboards, client portals, booking systems, and internal tools.
faq.q2: How long does it take to build a web app?
faq.a2: An MVP can be live in 4–6 weeks. A standard platform with payments, multi-language support, and CI/CD pipeline typically takes 8–12 weeks. Enterprise platforms with custom integrations and SLA take 12–20 weeks. Timelines depend on scope and feature complexity.
faq.q3: What technology stack do you use?
faq.a3: We build with Next.js, TypeScript, React, Supabase (PostgreSQL), Stripe, and Vercel. This battle-tested stack is used by leading companies worldwide and is ideal for fast, scalable, maintainable web applications.
faq.q4: Can you build a SaaS product for my business idea?
faq.a4: Absolutely! We specialize in SaaS MVPs for Swiss founders and businesses. We help scope your product, build the core features, and launch fast — so you can validate with real users before investing further.
faq.a5: Yes. Our Enterprise package includes ongoing maintenance. For MVP and Standard tiers, we offer monthly retainer plans starting at CHF 490/month covering security updates, bug fixes, performance monitoring, and priority support.
faq.q6: How do you handle data security and GDPR compliance?
faq.a6: We follow security best practices: row-level security on all databases, secure authentication (JWT/OAuth), encrypted data in transit and at rest, and GDPR-compliant data handling. Swiss businesses can deploy on EU/CH-based infrastructure.

faq.q1: Which e-commerce platform is best for Swiss businesses?

faq.a2: Yes! We integrate TWINT, PostFinance, Stripe, and standard credit/debit cards — the payment methods Swiss customers prefer. Our Growth and Enterprise packages include full Swiss payment gateway setup.
faq.q3: Can you build a multilingual store for the Swiss market?
faq.a3: Absolutely. Switzerland has four official languages. We build stores in German, French, Italian, and English with automatic currency switching (CHF, EUR, USD) and region-specific pricing if needed.
faq.q4: Will my online store rank on Google?
faq.a4: Yes! Our Growth and Enterprise packages include advanced SEO — product Schema markup, optimised category and collection pages, fast loading speeds, and sitemap configuration. We also integrate Google Shopping feeds.
faq.q5: How many products can my store handle?
faq.a5: Our Starter Shop handles up to 50 products. Growth Shop is unlimited. All packages include product import/export tools and inventory management. For high-volume stores, we can implement database-optimised architectures.
faq.q6: What support do you offer after the store launches?
faq.a6: Training is included in every package so you can manage your store yourself. We offer monthly maintenance plans for security updates, backups, and small changes. Our team is also available for custom feature additions as your business grows.

faq.subtitle: Social Media Marketing in Switzerland
faq.q1: How much does social media management cost in Switzerland?
faq.a1: Our Digital Starter plan is CHF 490/month (8 posts/month, 1 platform). Digital Growth is CHF 690/month (12 posts, 2 platforms, Stories). Digital Pro is CHF 990/month (16 posts, 3 platforms, full engagement management). All plans include AI-assisted content creation and monthly reporting.
faq.q2: How do you create content without a photoshoot?
faq.a2: You send us photos from your phone or business. We professionally enhance them using AI tools — background removal, colour correction, branding overlays, and upscaling. This keeps costs 60–70% lower than traditional agencies while delivering professional results.
faq.a3: We manage Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, and Google Business Profile. Each plan covers 1–3 platforms. Additional platforms are available as add-ons for CHF 99/month each. We'll recommend the right platforms based on where your customers spend time.
faq.q4: Can you also run paid ad campaigns?
faq.a4: Yes! We manage Meta Ads (Facebook + Instagram) from CHF 690/month and Google Ads from CHF 449/month. We also offer a combined bundle at CHF 699/month. Ad spend is billed separately directly by Meta and Google.
faq.q5: Do you write captions in German and other Swiss languages?
faq.a5: Yes! We create content in any language your audience speaks — German, English, French, Italian, or Portuguese. This is especially valuable for Swiss businesses serving multiple language regions.
faq.q6: How quickly can you start managing our social media?
faq.a6: We can onboard new clients within 1 week. After a kickoff call to understand your brand voice, target audience, and goals, we prepare your first month's content calendar for your approval before publishing anything.
faq.q7: What is cold email outreach and how does it differ from ads?
faq.a7: Cold email is direct outreach to specific businesses via personalised email sequences. Unlike ads — which show to broad audiences and require ongoing ad spend — cold email targets decision-makers directly at a fixed monthly fee with no cost per click. It works best for B2B offers where you know exactly who your ideal client is.
faq.q8: Can I combine cold email with social media or ads?
faq.a8: Yes — and it is a powerful combination. Cold email generates direct replies from targeted prospects, while social media builds awareness and trust over time. Clients who see your content before receiving an email are significantly more likely to reply. We can help you run both channels in a coordinated strategy.

faq.label: Common questions
faq.title: Pricing FAQ
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before making a decision.

items.q: How can you offer prices 40–60% below Zurich agencies?
items.a: As a solo technical founder powered by AI tools, there's no agency overhead — no account managers, no large offices, no sales teams. What you pay for is pure execution: design, code, and strategy. AI handles repetitive production tasks, which cuts delivery time without cutting quality.

None. Every price on this page is the full price. The only additions are: ad spend (billed directly by Meta/Google to you), AI platform costs for enterprise AI projects (also billed directly to you), and optional payment plan markups if you choose instalments.

items.q: Do I need to sign a long-term contract?
items.a: For one-time projects (websites, branding), there's no ongoing commitment — you pay, we deliver. For monthly services (ads, social media, care plans), we ask for a 3-month minimum commitment, then it's month-to-month. Cancel anytime with 30 days notice.

items.q: How do you maintain quality at these prices?
items.a: Speed and AI. Modern AI tools handle repetitive tasks like copywriting, image optimisation, and code boilerplate — dramatically cutting production time without affecting quality. You still get custom design, real strategy, and direct access to the founder throughout every project.

items.q: Can I combine services for a discount?
items.a: Yes. Bundling is already built into the pricing — the Meta + Google Bundle saves CHF 99/mo vs. buying each separately. For larger combinations (website + branding + care plan), reach out and we'll put together a custom quote tailored to your needs.
Lopes2Tech currently operates below the Swiss VAT registration threshold, so no VAT is charged. All prices shown are final — what you see is what you pay.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: Bank transfer (IBAN), Stripe (credit/debit card), and Twint for Swiss clients. For instalment plans, billing is automated monthly via Stripe. Invoices are issued in CHF.

items.q: What if I am not happy with the result?
items.a: Every project includes revision rounds — typically 2–3 depending on the package. We don't sign off until you're satisfied. My reputation is built on referrals, so your satisfaction is the business. If something is genuinely off-target, we fix it.

items.q: Is there a cheaper way to get started?
items.a: Yes — the Quick Wins section. A Website Audit for CHF 99 or a Google Business Boost for CHF 80 are perfect low-risk entry points. They deliver immediate value and let you experience how we work before committing to a larger project.

items.q: How accurate are the Zurich market averages shown?
items.a: Every market comparison on this page was researched against publicly listed prices from 12+ Swiss agencies including resign.ch, webtree.ch, peakline-agency.ch, cynatic.ch, digitalcreators.ch and others — cross-referenced in March 2026. These are documented figures, not estimates.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before starting.

items.question: Will this work for my industry?
items.answer: Cold email works in almost any B2B context where there is a clear decision-maker to reach. It works especially well for professional services (lawyers, accountants, clinics), agencies, consultants, software companies, and local businesses targeting other businesses. It is not suitable for B2C or highly regulated industries (finance, pharmaceuticals) without additional compliance steps.

items.question: How many replies can I realistically expect?
items.answer: Industry benchmark for well-targeted, personalised cold email campaigns is 3–8% reply rate. On 100 contacts that means 3–8 replies per month — not all of which will convert. Results depend heavily on offer clarity, target specificity, and how compelling your value proposition is. We are transparent about this upfront.

items.question: What is the setup fee for?
items.answer: The one-time CHF 150 setup fee covers: creating and configuring a sending subdomain (to protect your main domain), setting up SPF/DKIM/DMARC DNS records, connecting the inbox to Instantly.ai, and running a 2-week email warmup so your first campaign hits the inbox instead of spam. This is only charged once — never on renewals.

items.question: Will my main domain be at risk?
items.answer: No. We always use a sending subdomain (e.g., mail.yourdomain.com) — never your main domain. This completely isolates your primary email reputation from the campaign. In the unlikely event of any deliverability issues, your business email is unaffected.

items.question: How long before I see results?
items.answer: The first 2 weeks are spent on warmup (required for inbox placement). Campaigns typically go live in week 3. First replies usually start coming in within days of launch. Expect the first meaningful data and optimisation recommendations after the first full month.

items.question: Can I cancel after the minimum commitment?
items.answer: Yes. After the 2-month minimum, the service is month-to-month. Cancel any time with 30 days' notice — no annual contracts, no penalties.

items.question: Do I need to buy any tools or software?
items.answer: No. We handle Instantly.ai, the lead database, email verification, and all technical infrastructure. You do not need to purchase or set up anything yourself.
